violations.text: Observed cleaning product and cleaning cloth stored on equipment (handwashing sink in warewashing area), utensils, or single service items. PIC relocated cleaning product and cleaning cloth to designated area. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Observed several dry ingredient working containers not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Several TCS foods (see disposal form) were observed held at 45-61 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) The front half of the walk-in cooler was not holding adequate ambient temperature 2) The two flip top prep units were not holding adequate ambient temperature 3) The wall mounted sanitizer dispenser was dispensing only hot water which does not allow for adequate sanitizing/employee use New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
Soap was not available at a hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

A paper towel dispenser was observed above the food prep sink. A sink used for FOOD preparation or UTENSIL washing, or a service sink or curbed cleaning facility used for the disposal of mop water or similar wastes, may not be provided with the handwashing aids and devices required for a HANDWASHING SINK. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
Dressings (made in house) were observed held past their seven day date marking. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024

Quart containers were being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/08/2024

The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) The handle of a faucet of a handsink was not dispensing water 2) The sanitizer dispenser at the three bay sink was dispensing extremely hot water that did not allow for proper employee use Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sink & surface sanitizing solution for the three bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The area around the dumpster within the enclosure was observed with an accumulation of debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
Soap was not available at a hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

A paper towel dispenser was observed above the food prep sink. A sink used for FOOD preparation or UTENSIL washing, or a service sink or curbed cleaning facility used for the disposal of mop water or similar wastes, may not be provided with the handwashing aids and devices required for a HANDWASHING SINK. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Dish machine was taken out of service at time of inspection. PIC instructed to use three bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
Several foods (see disposal form) were observed held past their seven day date marking. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The following in-use utensils were observed improperly stored/used: 1) Large spoon observed stored with handle in direct contact with food 2) A plastic cup was observed being used as a scoop in a container of dry ingredients Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) Two hand sinks were not dispensing water (hot or cold) 2) The left handle of a faucet of a hand sink was not dispensing water 3) The sanitizer dispenser at the three bay sink was dispensing extremely hot water that did not allow for proper employee use New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sink and surface sanitizing solution for the three bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer have an accumulation of food debris. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The area around the dumpster within the enclosure was observed with an accumulation of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/15/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at ALL times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment to have multiple employees get certified in food safety. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023

Multiple foods were observed held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in on the side away from the door. Sauces in the mini fridge were observed at 50-54 degrees. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food in walk-in was moved to colder side and warmer side was adjusted at the time of inspection. Per PIC, unit had issues in the past. Unit to get serviced to ensure that it is working adequately. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
-The handle to the walk-in cooler was in poor repair. Handle to get replaced. -The mini fridge holding all the sauces was observed iced over. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the hood ventilation covers have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and grease. Per PIC, technician is supposed to come out to clean hood vents. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023

The ceiling tiles in the dining area is in poor repair (Water damage). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Establishment to repair ceiling to ensure no future leaks. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023
